item.description: President Trump has left for Beijing and a high-stakes summit with China’s President Xi. NBC Nightly News anchor Tom Llamas is there.

media:content.media:title: Trump heads to high-stakes summit in China
media:content.media:description: President Trump has left for Beijing and a high-stakes summit with China’s President Xi. NBC Nightly News anchor Tom Llamas is there.

item.title: Trump heads to high-stakes summit in China

item.description: Tom Llamas takes a Chinese electric vehicle for a test drive to see why brands like BYD are dominating the global market and why a 100% U.S. tariff is part of what’s keeping them out of American driveways.

media:content.media:title: Inside China’s battle for electric vehicle dominance
media:content.media:description: Tom Llamas takes a Chinese electric vehicle for a test drive to see why brands like BYD are dominating the global market and why a 100% U.S. tariff is part of what’s keeping them out of American driveways.

item.title: Inside China’s battle for electric vehicle dominance

item.dc:creator: Tom Llamas
Erin Tan
item.description: BEIJING — Old Cheng was waiting patiently at the bottom of a steep mountain ridge as the minivans carrying our news team snaked their way through the Yan mountains to meet him.

media:content.media:text: Construction workers at Phase 5 of the Great Wall reconstruction in Jiankou, China on Tuesday.
media:content.media:description: Construction workers at Phase 5 of the Great Wall reconstruction in Jiankou, China on Tuesday.

media:content.media:text: Top: NBC News' Tom Llamas, center, is guided through the forest beneath the Jiankou section of the Great Wall of China on Tuesday. Bottom: Cheng Yongmao, known as Old Cheng, directs his team of construction workers at the wall.
media:content.media:description: Construction workers at Phase 5 of the Great Wall reconstruction in Jiankou, China on Tuesday.

media:content.media:text: The Great Wall in Xiangshuihu, Huairou District on April 22, 2026.
media:content.media:description: Construction workers at Phase 5 of the Great Wall reconstruction in Jiankou, China on Tuesday.

media:content.media:text: Construction workers at Phase 5 of the Great Wall restoration at Xiangshuihu on Tuesday.
media:content.media:description: Construction workers at Phase 5 of the Great Wall reconstruction in Jiankou, China on Tuesday.

media:content.media:text: Jiankou is one of the most dangerous sections of the Great Wall, known for its steep, often unrestored terrain.
media:content.media:description: Construction workers at Phase 5 of the Great Wall reconstruction in Jiankou, China on Tuesday.

media:content.media:text: Restoration along the Jiankou section of the Great Wall on Tuesday.
media:content.media:description: Construction workers at Phase 5 of the Great Wall reconstruction in Jiankou, China on Tuesday.

item.title: The Great Wall is being restored one brick at a time

item.dc:creator: Babak Dehghanpisheh
item.description: While the Iranian regime has been battling its external enemies, Israel and the United States, it has also been ramping up a deadly crackdown on those seen as the enemy within.

media:content.media:description: The news site of the Iranian judiciary reported on April 5 that Mohammad Amin Biglari, pictured here, was hanged.

media:content.media:text: People gather during protest in Tehran, Iran.
media:content.media:description: The news site of the Iranian judiciary reported on April 5 that Mohammad Amin Biglari, pictured here, was hanged.
The news site of the Iranian judiciary reported on April 5 that Mohammad Amin Biglari, pictured here, was hanged.

item.title: The war is on hold, but Iran’s internal crackdown is only deepening

item.dc:creator: Mosheh Gains
Courtney Kube
Monica Alba
item.description: The U.S. military is considering officially re-naming the war with Iran to “Operation Sledgehammer” if the current ceasefire collapses

media:content.media:text: A woman walks near a billboard featuring an image of the late Supreme Leader of Iran, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, in Tehran
media:content.media:description: A woman walks near a billboard featuring an image of the late supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, in Tehran, Iran, on Monday.

media:content.media:text: A cargo ship docked in front of stone levees at a port.
media:content.media:description: A woman walks near a billboard featuring an image of the late supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, in Tehran, Iran, on Monday.

item.title: Pentagon considering renaming Iran war ‘Sledgehammer’ if ceasefire collapses

item.dc:creator: Allan Smith
item.description: President Donald Trump told reporters on Tuesday that he is not weighing the economic burden of the Iran war on everyday Americans when negotiating a deal with the country’s leadership

media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump arrives for a "Rose Garden Club" dinner in honor of Police Week at the White House on May 11, 2026.

item.title: Trump says he’s not thinking about Americans’ finances ‘even a little bit’ in Iran talks

item.dc:creator: Jared Perlo
item.description: OpenEvidence, an AI-powered medical search tool, has become a fast friend to America’s doctors and is now used by nearly two-thirds of physicians.

media:content.media:text: A patient lies in a hospital bed with a pick line attached to their arm, which is connected to a glowing computer chip that is connected to various IV bags and medical screens.
media:content.media:description: Most U.S. doctors are using OpenEvidence to inform medical decision-making.
Most U.S. doctors are using OpenEvidence to inform medical decision-making.

media:content.media:text: OpenEvidence looks similar to many mainstream AI platforms, but tuned for medicine.
media:content.media:description: Most U.S. doctors are using OpenEvidence to inform medical decision-making.
Most U.S. doctors are using OpenEvidence to inform medical decision-making.

media:content.media:text: Dr. Hannah Galvin of Cambridge Health Alliance.
media:content.media:description: Most U.S. doctors are using OpenEvidence to inform medical decision-making.
Most U.S. doctors are using OpenEvidence to inform medical decision-making.

item.title: Most U.S. doctors are quietly using this AI tool. Few patients know about it.

item.dc:creator: Bracey Harris
Abigail Brooks
item.description: In a state with a long history of arresting pregnant women, one mom in custody says she was left to fend for herself.

media:content.media:description: Tiffany McElroy in downtown Dothan, Ala.
Tiffany McElroy in downtown Dothan, Ala.
Tiffany McElroy in downtown Dothan, Ala.
Tiffany McElroy in downtown Dothan, Ala.

item.title: Alabama jail staff didn’t help when she went into labor — other inmates did, lawsuit says

item.dc:creator: Scott Wong
Melanie Zanona
item.description: The Congressional Black Caucus is expected to dramatically lose membership because of the Supreme Court’s redistricting ruling.

media:content.media:description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries, D-N.Y., with other members of the Congressional Black Caucus on April 29.

item.title: Dems warn a third of Congressional Black Caucus could be wiped out by redistricting wars

item.description: One of the passengers from the cruise ship hit with a deadly outbreak of Hantavirus posted a video showing what the accommodations are like at the special medical facility in Omaha where passengers are being monitored. NBC News’ Camila Bernal reports.

media:content.media:title: Inside look at quarantine facility for cruise ship passengers
media:content.media:description: One of the passengers from the cruise ship hit with a deadly outbreak of Hantavirus posted a video showing what the accommodations are like at the special medical facility in Omaha where passengers are being monitored. NBC News’ Camila Bernal reports.

item.title: Inside look at quarantine facility for cruise ship passengers

item.dc:creator: Yuliya Talmazan
item.description: College students across Russia are being lured to join the country’s drone forces in Ukraine, but rights activists say the offer could be a trap.

media:content.media:description: A billboard in St. Petersburg, Russia, advertising a contract in Russia's drone forces. The sign reads: "The new ones. The irreplaceable ones. Russian unmanned troops."
A billboard in St. Petersburg, Russia, advertising a contract in Russia's drone forces. The sign reads: "The new ones. The irreplaceable ones. Russian unmanned troops."

media:content.media:text: FILE PHOTO: People walk under an ad promoting contract military service in Russian army, in St Petersburg
media:content.media:description: A billboard in St. Petersburg, Russia, advertising a contract in Russia's drone forces. The sign reads: "The new ones. The irreplaceable ones. Russian unmanned troops."
A billboard in St. Petersburg, Russia, advertising a contract in Russia's drone forces. The sign reads: "The new ones. The irreplaceable ones. Russian unmanned troops."

item.title: Inside Russia’s push to recruit students as drone pilots in Ukraine

item.description: Former FBI director James Comey joins "Here's the Scoop" to talk about the politicization of the Department of Justice, saying that he disagrees with the criticism that his 2016 investigation of Hillary Clinton started the shift.

media:content.media:title: Comey says he doesn't regret Clinton investigation
media:content.media:description: Former FBI director James Comey joins "Here's the Scoop" to talk about the politicization of the Department of Justice, saying that he disagrees with the criticism that his 2016 investigation of Hillary Clinton started the shift.

item.title: Comey says he doesn't regret Clinton investigation

item.description: Speaking to reporters as he left the White House for his trip to China this afternoon, President Trump laid out his plan to discuss the Iran war with Chinese President Xi Jinping.

media:content.media:title: Trump says he plans to have 'long talk' with Xi about Iran
media:content.media:description: Speaking to reporters as he left the White House for his trip to China this afternoon, President Trump laid out his plan to discuss the Iran war with Chinese President Xi Jinping.

item.title: Trump says he plans to have 'long talk' with Xi about Iran

item.description: As album rollouts continue to evolve, fans recently attempted to melt a giant ice block in the middle of Toronto as part of Drake's "Iceman" announcement. But how did we get here? NBC News' Gadi Schwartz takes a look back at other memorable rollouts, and how they have advanced through the years.

media:content.media:title: The evolution of album rollouts
media:content.media:description: As album rollouts continue to evolve, fans recently attempted to melt a giant ice block in the middle of Toronto as part of Drake's "Iceman" announcement. But how did we get here? NBC News' Gadi Schwartz takes a look back at other memorable rollouts, and how they have advanced through the years.

item.title: The evolution of album rollouts
